articles.headline: A-League Women stocks up, stocks down: Glory come from behind twice; WSW struggle
articles.description: Perth Glory came from behind twice to beat the Newcastle Jets, while the Western Sydney Wanderers were toothless in front of goal.

images.name: ALW RD 18 stocks [1296x729]
images.caption: Perth Glory came from behind to defeat the Newcastle Jets, while the Western Sydney Wanderers struggled in front of goal.

images.name: What does Micah's withdrawal mean for Matildas goalkeepers?
images.caption: With Teagan Micah withdrawing from the Matildas squad due to concussion, ESPN's The Far Post discusses what this means for the goalkeeping unit.

articles.headline: A-League Women stocks up, stocks down: Pearson breaks long-standing Kerr record
articles.description: Willa Pearson broke a long-standing Sam Kerr record, while the Wellington Phoenix were shooting but not scoring.

images.name: ALW stocks rd 17 [1296x729]
images.caption: Willa Pearson broke a long-standing Sam Kerr record, while the Wellington Phoenix were shooting but not scoring.

images.name: Pearson becomes youngest goalscorer in A-League Women history
images.caption: 15-year-old Willa Pearson has broken Sam Kerr's record to become the youngest goalscorer in A-league women history in Sydney's 4-0 win over Brisbane.

articles.headline: Teen gun breaks Kerr record as Sydney beat Roar in ALW
articles.description: Teenager Willa Pearson has broken Sam Kerr's 17-year-old record in becoming the youngest player to score in the A-League Women as Sydney FC beat Brisbane Roar 4-0.

images.name: Willa Pearson [1296x729]
images.caption: Already the youngest ever ALW player, having debuted as a 14-year-old, Pearson became the youngest goal scorer at 15 years and 51 days.

images.name: Amy Chessari scores as WSW end derby drought
images.caption: The Western Sydney Wanderers defeat Sydney FC for the first time in 2234 days and 12 Sydney derbies.

articles.headline: A-League Women stocks up, stocks down: Woods' stock rise, Roar ship five unanswered
articles.description: Makala Woods' stocks continue to rise at Wellington Phoenix, while Brisbane Roar shipped five unanswered goals.

images.name: ALW stocks rd 16 [1296x729]
images.caption: Makala Woods' stocks continue to rise while Brisbane Roar shipped five unanswered goals.

images.name: Holly Furphy lifts Victory over Newcastle
images.caption: A sensational strike from Holly Furphy sees Melbourne Victory outlast the Newcastle Jets 2-1.

articles.headline: A-League Women stocks up, stocks down: Derby delight for WSW, era ends at Sydney FC
articles.description: It was derby delight for the Western Sydney Wanderers while Sydney FC witnessed the end of an era in more ways than one.

images.name: ALW STOCKS RD 15 [1296x729]
images.caption: It was derby delight for the Western Sydney Wanderers while Sydney FC witnessed the end of an era in more ways than one.

images.name: Amy Chessari scores as WSW end derby drought
images.caption: The Western Sydney Wanderers defeat Sydney FC for the first time in 2234 days and 12 Sydney derbies.

articles.headline: Sydney FC confirm Ante Juric's exit after derby defeat to WSW
articles.description: Ante Juric's glory-laden reign in charge of Sydney FC's A-League Women side is over after the coach agreed to part ways with the Sky Blues.

images.name: Amy Chessari scores as WSW end derby drought
images.caption: The Western Sydney Wanderers defeat Sydney FC for the first time in 2234 days and 12 Sydney derbies.

notes.headline: 2nd Leg - Sydney FC  advance 2-1 on aggregate

header.gameNote: 2nd Leg - Sydney FC  advance 2-1 on aggregate

keyEvents.text: Isabel Gomez (Central Coast Mariners Women) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Goal!  Sydney FC 1, Central Coast Mariners Women 0. Mackenzie Hawkesby (Sydney FC) right footed shot from the centre of the box to the bottom left corner.
Goal!  Sydney FC 1, Central Coast Mariners Women 1. Rola Badawiya (Central Coast Mariners Women) right footed shot from the centre of the box to the bottom right corner. Assisted by Bianca Galic.
Faye Bryson (Central Coast Mariners Women) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Substitution, Central Coast Mariners Women. Annalise Rasmussen replaces Wurigumula.
Substitution, Central Coast Mariners Women. Peta Trimis replaces Paige Hayward.
Substitution, Sydney FC. Shea Connors replaces Princess Ibini.
Jazmin Wardlow (Central Coast Mariners Women) is shown the yellow card for hand ball.
Substitution, Sydney FC. Caley Tallon-Henniker replaces Indiana dos Santos.
Substitution, Sydney FC. Lucy Johnson replaces Mackenzie Hawkesby.
Second Half ends, Sydney FC 1, Central Coast Mariners Women 1.
